Home › Apps › Clash Solver
Revit add-in

Clash Solver

Stops reporting clashes. Starts solving them.

Ingests your ACC clash report, proposes a resolution for every clash, and applies the resolutions you accept — straight into the live model.

Triagereal clashes vs ghost in minutes· ~80%auto-proposed resolutions· One-clickaccept and apply to the live model· Auditableevery resolution is a reversible transaction

Run Clash Solver → See related apps

Why use Clash Solver

Connect Clash Solver to your ACC project. It pulls the latest clash report, deduplicates against the previous run, and triages: real clash vs ghost (model-link drift, family swap, view-filter mismatch). For each real clash it proposes a resolution — re-route the smaller service, raise the ceiling void in the affected zone, swap a fitting, etc. — and shows you what the model would look like after.

You accept, refine, or reject each proposal. Accepted resolutions are applied to the live model as auditable Revit transactions (one-click undo). Rejected ones go into a 'why we kept this' log so the next sync doesn't propose the same thing.

For a typical 800-clash weekly report on a coordination-heavy scheme, Clash Solver triages to 150–200 real clashes in minutes, proposes resolutions for ~80% of them, and lets you process the rest in roughly half the time of a manual triage. Most teams see their open clash count drop week-on-week for the first time.

Clash Solver will be the only tool that closes the clash loop — detection AND resolution, audited and reversible, on your live model.

The shift

What working looks like today versus with Clash Solver.

Before

Clash reports are weekly noise: 800 clashes flagged, 200 actually need solving, half the team spends Friday afternoon working out which is which. The model never quite catches up.

After Clash Solver

Clash Solver triages the report (real vs noise), proposes a resolution per real clash, and applies the ones you accept. The clash count drops weekly instead of climbing.

What makes it different

Features

What Clash Solver does. Each line is a shipped capability.

Clash Solver capabilities

ACC live import Pulls the latest clash report from Autodesk Construction Cloud automatically.
›
Ghost-clash filtering Recognises link drift, family swap, view-filter mismatch as ghosts, not real clashes.
›
Resolution proposals Re-route, fitting swap, zone raise, or coordinate-change, with a model preview.
›
One-click apply Accepted resolutions executed as auditable Revit transactions.
›
Reject + reason Rejected proposals logged so the next run doesn't re-propose the same thing.
›
Zone-level batch Fix a whole zone in one pass instead of clash-by-clash.
›
Status dashboard Open / triaged / resolved / rejected counts per project, per discipline.
›
Weekly digest Summary of what got resolved, what's stuck, what needs a human call.
›

Install & system

Where Clash Solver runs, what you need, and how to get it.

Revit add-in Revit 2022 → 2026 · Windows · loaded with the Revit Copilot or QA Manager.
Web dashboard Triage + accept proposals from the browser; applies happen in Revit when you're next in.

Requirements

  • Revit 2022+ for the apply step.
  • An Adelphos account with project admin rights.
  • ACC connection for clash report import.

Sign-in

Microsoft 365 SSO recommended (Revit + ACC + email in one).

Run Clash Solver → Read the docs first

Who it's for

Coordination-heavy commercial + healthcare schemesPre-issue clash gateWeekly triage automation

Related

Feature 3D Plantroom Designer An agentic 3D modeller that designs the plantroom, sizes the equipment, routes the services, and hands you a Revit model ready for issue. App Revit Copilot Talk to Revit. The Copilot understands your model, applies your standards, and runs the 163 commands and 191 tools that ship with the platform. App Adelphos Chat Same agent. Same project context. Browser, phone, or desktop. The Adelphos Chat is the agent untethered from any one tool.

Frequently asked questions

Quick answers AI engines (and humans) can pull from.

What is Clash Solver?

Ingests your ACC clash report, proposes a resolution for every clash, and applies the resolutions you accept — straight into the live model.

What does Clash Solver do?

Connect Clash Solver to your ACC project. It pulls the latest clash report, deduplicates against the previous run, and triages: real clash vs ghost (model-link drift, family swap, view-filter mismatch). For each real clash it proposes a resolution — re-route the smaller service, raise the ceiling void in the affected zone, swap a fitting, etc. — and shows you what the model would look like after.

How is Clash Solver different from the manual workflow?

Without Clash Solver: Clash reports are weekly noise: 800 clashes flagged, 200 actually need solving, half the team spends Friday afternoon working out which is which. The model never quite catches up. With Clash Solver: Clash Solver triages the report (real vs noise), proposes a resolution per real clash, and applies the ones you accept. The clash count drops weekly instead of climbing.

How much time does Clash Solver save?

Headline outcomes for Clash Solver: Triage (real clashes vs ghost in minutes); ~80% (auto-proposed resolutions); One-click (accept and apply to the live model); Auditable (every resolution is a reversible transaction).

Who is Clash Solver for?

Coordination-heavy commercial + healthcare schemes; Pre-issue clash gate; Weekly triage automation.

Run Clash Solver.

Built into the Adelphos platform. Self-serve where possible, managed where useful.
